items.question: Is this HEIC converter safe to use?
items.answer: Yes, absolutely. All file conversion happens directly in your browser using JavaScript. Your files are never uploaded to any server — your photos remain 100% private on your device.

items.question: What is a HEIC file?
items.answer: HEIC (High Efficiency Image Container) is the default photo format on Apple iPhones and iPads since iOS 11. It stores photos at roughly half the size of JPEG at the same quality, but many devices and apps can't open it — which is why converting to JPG or PNG is so useful.

items.question: How do I open HEIC files on Windows or Android?
items.answer: Windows and most Android devices can't open HEIC files natively. The fastest fix is to convert the HEIC file to JPG right here — no software to install. On Windows 10/11 you can alternatively install the HEIF Image Extensions from the Microsoft Store.

items.question: Why are my iPhone photos saved as HEIC?
items.answer: Since iOS 11, iPhones save photos as HEIC by default to save storage space. If you prefer JPEG, go to Settings > Camera > Formats and choose 'Most Compatible' — or simply convert your existing HEIC photos here whenever you need them.

items.question: Is there a file size limit?
items.answer: There is no strict file size limit since all processing happens in your browser. Very large files (over 50MB) may just take a little longer to convert depending on your device.

items.question: Do I need to create an account?
items.answer: No. OpenMyHEIC is completely free with no registration, no account, and no limit on the number of conversions.

jpg.question: How do I convert HEIC to JPG?
jpg.answer: Drag and drop your HEIC files into the converter above (or click to browse), adjust the quality if you like, and press Convert. Your JPG downloads instantly — the whole conversion happens in your browser.

jpg.question: How can I change HEIC to JPG without installing software?
jpg.answer: That's exactly what this page does. There's nothing to install and nothing to upload: the HEIC file is converted to JPG locally in your browser, on Windows, Mac, Linux, Android or iPhone.

jpg.question: Does converting HEIC to JPG lose quality?
jpg.answer: JPG is a compressed format, but at the default 92% quality setting the difference is invisible to the eye. You can raise the quality slider to 100% for maximum fidelity or lower it for smaller files.

jpg.question: What is the difference between HEIC and JPEG?
jpg.answer: HEIC compresses photos about 50% better than JPEG at the same quality and supports extras like Live Photos and 16-bit color. JPEG's advantage is universal compatibility — every device, browser and app can open it. That's why converting HEIC to JPG is often necessary.

jpg.question: How do I convert HEIC to JPG on Windows or Mac?
jpg.answer: This converter works on both — nothing to install. If you'd rather use built-in tools, check our step-by-step guides for Windows and Mac linked in the footer below.

jpg.question: How do I convert HEIC to JPG on iPhone?
jpg.answer: Open this page in Safari on your iPhone and convert directly. To stop your iPhone from taking HEIC photos in the first place, go to Settings > Camera > Formats and select 'Most Compatible'.

png.question: How do I convert HEIC to PNG?
png.answer: Drop your HEIC file into the converter above and press Convert. The PNG file downloads instantly — no upload, no signup, and it works in any modern browser.

png.question: Is HEIC to PNG conversion lossless?
png.answer: Yes. PNG uses lossless compression, so every pixel of the decoded HEIC image is preserved exactly. It's the best choice when maximum quality matters.

png.question: Does PNG keep transparency from HEIC?
png.answer: Yes. If your HEIC image contains an alpha channel (transparency), converting to PNG preserves it — unlike JPG, which flattens transparent areas.

png.question: Why is my PNG file bigger than the original HEIC?
png.answer: HEIC uses very efficient lossy compression while PNG is lossless, so PNG files are typically 3-10x larger. That's the trade-off for perfect quality and universal compatibility.

png.question: Is this HEIC to PNG converter free?
png.answer: Completely free — no watermarks, no registration, no limits on the number of files you convert.

pdf.question: How do I convert HEIC to PDF?
pdf.answer: Select one or more HEIC files above and press Convert. You'll get a single PDF document containing your photos — created entirely in your browser, with no upload.

pdf.question: Can I combine multiple HEIC photos into one PDF?
pdf.answer: Yes. This converter accepts multiple HEIC files at once and merges them into a single multi-page PDF — handy for receipts, documents and photo collections.

pdf.question: Can I turn scanned HEIC photos into a PDF?
pdf.answer: Yes. If you photographed documents with your iPhone, just drop those HEIC files here and they'll be combined into a clean PDF you can share or archive.

pdf.question: Is the HEIC to PDF converter free?
pdf.answer: Yes — free, unlimited and private. Files never leave your device, so even sensitive documents stay safe.

pdf.question: Will my photo quality be preserved in the PDF?
pdf.answer: Yes. Images are embedded at high quality, and you can adjust the quality slider before converting to balance sharpness against file size.

batch.question: How do I batch convert HEIC to JPG?
batch.answer: Select all your HEIC files at once (Ctrl/Cmd-click or drag a whole selection), choose JPG as the output format and press Convert. Every file is converted in your browser and bundled into a ZIP.

batch.question: How many HEIC files can I convert at once?
batch.answer: There's no fixed limit — hundreds of files work fine. Since conversion runs on your own device, very large batches simply take a bit longer.

batch.question: Can I bulk convert HEIC to JPG for free?
batch.answer: Yes. Bulk conversion is completely free with no file limits, no watermarks and no registration — unlike most desktop bulk converters.

batch.question: How do I download all the converted files?
batch.answer: When the batch finishes, press 'Download ZIP' and you'll get a single ZIP archive containing every converted image.

batch.question: Can I batch convert HEIC to PNG or WebP too?
batch.answer: Yes. Before converting, choose PNG (lossless) or WebP (smallest files) as the output format instead of JPG.

viewer.question: How do I open a HEIC file without installing anything?
viewer.answer: Drag your HEIC files onto this page. The viewer decodes them in your browser and shows them instantly — no codecs, no apps, no account. It works on Windows 10 and 11, Mac, Linux, and Chromebooks.

viewer.question: Are my photos uploaded to a server?
viewer.answer: No. Everything happens locally in your browser using JavaScript. Your photos never leave your device, which makes this viewer safe for private pictures.

viewer.question: Can I view multiple HEIC files at once?
viewer.answer: Yes — select or drop as many files as you want and they'll appear in a gallery. Click any photo to see it full size in a new tab.

viewer.question: Can I save the photos as JPG?
viewer.answer: Yes. Every photo in the gallery has a JPG download button. For whole folders, the batch converter downloads everything as a single ZIP.

viewer.question: Why does Windows say it needs a codec to open HEIC?
viewer.answer: Windows needs the HEIF Image Extensions (and often the paid HEVC codec) to decode HEIC natively. This viewer skips that entirely by decoding the image in your browser.

privacy.metaDescription: Learn how OpenMyHEIC protects your privacy. All file conversions happen in your browser — we never upload, store, or access your photos.
privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.introText: At OpenMyHEIC.com, we are committed to protecting your privacy. This Privacy Policy explains how we handle information when you use our HEIC file conversion service. Our core principle is simple: your files never leave your device.
privacy.dataCollectionText: OpenMyHEIC is designed to process files entirely in your browser. We do NOT collect, upload, store, or have access to any files you convert. We may collect anonymous usage data such as page views, browser type, and general geographic region through standard web analytics tools to improve our service. No personally identifiable information is collected through the conversion process.
privacy.cookiesText: We use essential cookies to ensure the proper functioning of our website, such as remembering your language preference. Third-party services like Google AdSense may use cookies to serve personalized ads based on your browsing history. You can manage cookie preferences through your browser settings. By using our website, you consent to the use of cookies as described in this policy.
privacy.thirdPartyText: We use Google AdSense to display advertisements on our website. Google AdSense may use cookies and web beacons to collect information about your visits to this and other websites in order to provide relevant advertisements. Google's use of advertising cookies enables it and its partners to serve ads based on your visit to our site and/or other sites on the Internet. You may opt out of personalized advertising by visiting Google's Ads Settings (https://adssettings.google.com).
privacy.userRightsText: Since we do not collect personal data through our conversion service, there is typically no personal data for us to manage on your behalf. However, if you believe we hold any personal information about you, you have the right to: request access to your data, request correction of inaccurate data, request deletion of your data, and object to processing of your data. To exercise any of these rights, please contact us at contact@openmyheic.com.
privacy.changesText: We may update this Privacy Policy from time to time. Any changes will be posted on this page with an updated revision date. We encourage you to review this policy periodically to stay informed about how we protect your information.
privacy.contactText: If you have any questions about this Privacy Policy, please contact us at contact@openmyheic.com.

terms.metaDescription: Read the Terms of Use for OpenMyHEIC.com. Understand the conditions for using our free, browser-based HEIC file conversion service.

We do not upload, store, or process your files on any server. The service converts HEIC/HEIF image files to various formats including JPG, PNG, WebP, BMP, GIF, and PDF.
terms.usageText: You agree to use OpenMyHEIC.com only for lawful purposes. You must not use this service to convert files that you do not have the right to process, or for any purpose that violates applicable local, national, or international laws. You are solely responsible for the content of the files you convert.
terms.intellectualPropertyText: The OpenMyHEIC.com website, including its design, logos, text, and code, is protected by intellectual property laws. You may not reproduce, distribute, or create derivative works from our website content without prior written permission. Your converted files remain entirely your property — we claim no rights over any files you process using our service.
terms.limitationText: OpenMyHEIC.com is provided "as is" without warranties of any kind, either express or implied. We do not guarantee that the service will be uninterrupted, error-free, or that conversion results will meet your specific requirements. In no event shall OpenMyHEIC.com be liable for any indirect, incidental, special, or consequential damages arising from the use or inability to use our service.
terms.disclaimerText: While we strive to provide accurate and reliable conversions, we make no warranties regarding the quality, accuracy, or completeness of any converted files. Users should verify the output of conversions for their specific use cases. We are not responsible for any data loss that may occur during the conversion process.
terms.changesText: We reserve the right to modify these Terms of Use at any time. Changes will be effective immediately upon posting on this page. Your continued use of the service after any changes constitutes acceptance of the new terms. We encourage you to review these terms periodically.
terms.governingLawText: These Terms of Use shall be governed by and construed in accordance with applicable laws. Any disputes arising from these terms or the use of our service shall be resolved through appropriate legal channels.

contact.privacyReminderText: Remember, OpenMyHEIC processes all files locally in your browser. If you're experiencing conversion issues, please include your browser name and version in your message — but never send us your actual HEIC files, as we don't need them to troubleshoot.

viewer.h1: HEIC Viewer — Open and View HEIC Files Online
viewer.intro: Can't open your iPhone photos? Drop your HEIC files below and see them instantly — no software to install, nothing uploaded.
viewer.dropTitle: Drop your HEIC files here to view them
viewer.aboutTitle: Why can't I open HEIC files?
viewer.aboutText1: HEIC is the photo format iPhones use by default since iOS 11. It saves storage, but Windows 10, many Android devices, older software, and most websites can't display it without extra codecs. That's why a photo that looks fine on your iPhone shows an error on your PC.
viewer.aboutText2: This HEIC viewer decodes your photos directly in your browser using JavaScript — your files never leave your device. It works on any system with a modern browser: Windows, Mac, Linux, Chromebooks. View your photos, check them full size, and download the ones you need as JPG — or convert entire folders with the batch converter.

sections.h2: How to convert HEIC to JPG
p: Converting HEIC to JPG is simple: drag your iPhone photos into the box above, wait a second while your browser does the work, and download ready-to-use JPG files. Nothing is uploaded — the entire HEIC to JPG conversion runs locally on your device, so your photos stay private. You can change one photo or dozens at a time, and the JPG files you get open on Windows, Android, older software and every social network.

JPEG (JPG) is the most universally supported image format in the world. That's why converting HEIC to JPG is the quickest way to fix photos that won't open, won't upload, or show up as a grey box on a PC. Unlike installing codecs or extra apps, this tool works in any modern browser with no signup.

sections.h2: Why iPhone photos are HEIC — and when to convert
p: Since iOS 11, iPhones and iPads save photos as HEIC by default because it stores the same quality at roughly half the file size. The downside is compatibility: email attachments, Windows 10, many Android phones and most websites still expect JPG. Converting HEIC to JPG gives you a copy that works everywhere while keeping your originals untouched.

If you'd rather stop dealing with HEIC altogether, open Settings > Camera > Formats on your iPhone and pick 'Most Compatible' to shoot straight to JPEG. For every photo you already have, this free HEIC to JPG converter turns them compatible in seconds.

sections.h2: Convert HEIC to PNG with lossless quality
p: Use the converter above to change HEIC to PNG directly in your browser — free, private and with no upload. PNG uses lossless compression, so every pixel of your iPhone photo is preserved exactly. That makes HEIC to PNG the right choice when you need maximum quality for editing, screenshots, diagrams, logos or images with sharp text and fine detail.

PNG also supports transparency (an alpha channel), which JPG cannot. If you plan to place a photo over a coloured background, build a graphic, or feed the image into design software, converting HEIC to PNG keeps the flexibility you need. Every conversion happens locally, so even high-resolution files stay on your device.

sections.h2: HEIC to PNG vs HEIC to JPG — which should you pick?
p: Choose PNG when quality and transparency matter more than file size — editing, print, graphics and archiving. Choose JPG when you want the smallest file for sharing, uploading or storing lots of photos, since JPG files are far smaller than PNG for typical photographs.

You don't have to decide blindly: convert the same HEIC file both ways and compare. This tool supports HEIC to PNG, JPG, WebP, PDF, GIF and BMP, so you can match every photo to exactly the format you need without installing anything.

sections.h2: Convert HEIC to PDF and combine multiple photos
p: Turn one HEIC photo — or a whole batch — into a single PDF right in your browser. Add your files above, arrange them, and the tool builds a clean HEIC to PDF document you can download instantly. Because everything runs locally, your images are never uploaded, which matters for receipts, IDs, contracts and other sensitive scans.

PDF is the universal document format: it opens identically on any phone, computer or printer and keeps several images together in the right order. Converting HEIC to PDF is ideal for emailing a set of photos as one attachment, printing, or submitting scanned documents that started life as iPhone pictures.

sections.h2: Why convert HEIC to PDF instead of JPG
p: A single PDF is easier to share and archive than a folder full of separate images. When you need to send five photos to an office, a teacher or a client, one HEIC to PDF file looks professional and can't be reordered or lost. PDFs also print predictably at the size you set.

Keep JPG for the web and social media, and use PDF whenever the photos belong together as a document. This free HEIC to PDF converter handles both single images and multi-page documents, with no watermark and no page limit.

sections.h2: Convert HEIC to WebP for faster websites
p: WebP is Google's modern image format, built to make web pages load faster. Converting HEIC to WebP gives you files that are noticeably smaller than JPG or PNG at the same visual quality — perfect for websites, blogs, online stores and anywhere page speed and Core Web Vitals matter. Drop your HEIC files above and download optimized WebP images in seconds, with no upload.

Every major browser — Chrome, Firefox, Edge, Safari and Android — now supports WebP, so you get smaller images without sacrificing compatibility for modern web use. The whole HEIC to WebP conversion runs in your browser, so even large photo sets stay private on your device.

sections.h2: When to choose WebP over JPG or PNG
p: Pick WebP when your goal is smaller files and faster loading on the web: it combines JPG-style compression with PNG-style transparency in one format. For photos you'll publish online, HEIC to WebP typically cuts file size by 25–35% versus JPG.

Stick with JPG when you need the broadest possible compatibility with old software, or PNG when you need guaranteed lossless quality. For everything web-facing, WebP is usually the smartest choice — and this free converter lets you compare all three from the same HEIC file.

sections.h2: Convert HEIC to GIF and animate Live Photos
p: GIF is the format for simple animations and short looping clips that play anywhere. Converting HEIC to GIF lets you turn iPhone Live Photos and image sequences into shareable animations, or extract a single frame as a lightweight image. Add your files above and download your GIF instantly — free, private, and processed entirely in your browser.

Because GIFs play automatically in messages, chats, email and social feeds without a video player, they're the easiest way to share a moment that moves. Every HEIC to GIF conversion happens locally, so nothing is uploaded to a server.

sections.h2: GIF vs JPG for iPhone photos
p: Use GIF when you want animation or a small, universally playable clip; use JPG for a normal still photo you'll upload or print. GIF uses a limited colour palette, so it's ideal for motion and graphics rather than high-fidelity photographs.

If you only need a single crisp still, convert HEIC to JPG or PNG instead. This tool supports all of them, so you can pick GIF for movement and a photo format for detail — whatever each shot calls for.

sections.h2: Convert HEIC to BMP — fully uncompressed
p: BMP (bitmap) stores every pixel without compression, giving you a raw, uncompressed copy of your image. Converting HEIC to BMP is useful when older Windows software, certain editing tools, or legacy systems specifically require BMP input, or when you want an image with zero compression artefacts. Drop your HEIC files above and download BMP files directly, with no upload and no signup.

Because BMP keeps all image data intact, the files are large — but that's exactly the point when maximum fidelity or compatibility with old programs matters more than file size. The HEIC to BMP conversion runs locally in your browser, so even big bitmaps stay on your device.

sections.h2: When BMP makes sense — and when it doesn't
p: Choose BMP only when a specific application demands it or you need an uncompressed working file. For everyday use — sharing, uploading, storing or emailing photos — BMP files are impractically large.

For those cases convert HEIC to JPG for the smallest universal file, PNG for lossless quality with a reasonable size, or WebP for the web. This free tool converts HEIC to BMP, JPG, PNG, WebP, GIF and PDF, so you always have the right format on hand.

sections.h2: Open and view HEIC files without installing anything
p: This free HEIC viewer lets you open and view HEIC photos online instantly — no software, no plugins, no upload. Drag your iPhone photos into the box above and they're decoded right in your browser, so you can finally see the pictures that show up as a grey box or an error on Windows, Android and older programs. Your files never leave your device.

Being able to open HEIC files on any computer is useful when you're on a borrowed or work PC, a Chromebook, or a Linux machine where installing codecs isn't an option. The viewer works anywhere with a modern browser.

sections.h2: View now, convert when you need to
p: Once you can see your HEIC photos, download any of them as JPG in one click, or send the whole set to the batch converter to turn a full folder into JPG, PNG or PDF at once. Viewing and converting use the same private, in-browser engine.

If HEIC files keep catching you out, remember you can switch your iPhone to shoot JPEG under Settings > Camera > Formats > Most Compatible. Until then, this HEIC viewer opens whatever you already have.

sections.h2: Batch convert HEIC files in bulk
p: Need to convert a whole folder of iPhone photos? This batch HEIC converter lets you bulk convert many files at once — add dozens of HEIC files, choose JPG, PNG or WebP, and download them all together as a single ZIP. Everything is processed in your browser, so nothing is uploaded and your photos stay private no matter how many you convert.

Batch conversion saves the tedium of doing photos one by one. Whether you're clearing out a camera roll, preparing images for a website, or making a phone's worth of pictures compatible with Windows, bulk HEIC conversion handles the lot in a single pass.

Select or drag in all your HEIC files, pick an output format and quality, and start — a progress bar shows each file as it completes, and any that fail are flagged so nothing is silently lost. When it's done, one download gives you a ZIP with every converted image.

Because the batch converter runs locally, there's no file-count limit imposed by a server and no waiting on uploads. For single photos you can still use the standard HEIC to JPG, PNG or PDF tools; for everything else, batch is the fastest way to convert HEIC in bulk.
